---
title: "COP31 Climate Agreements Simulation"
subtitle: "Participant Rules and Procedures"
author: "PA 388K/345 Climate Policy — LBJ School, UT Austin"
date: "April 22, 2026"
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    fontsize: 11pt
    geometry: margin=1in
    colorlinks: true
    toc: false
---

# Overview

You are negotiating as a country delegation at COP31 in Antalya, Turkey. The goal is to reach a multilateral agreement that limits global warming to **2°C above pre-industrial levels** by 2100 while managing your country's abatement costs, climate damages, and financial obligations.

The simulation runs two rounds of negotiation. Your commitments are entered into a climate model that produces immediate temperature feedback. What you agree to in this room determines the outcome.

The United States is not at the table. Its actions will not be irrelevant.

---

# Your Proposal — Three Parameters

Each country submits three numbers at the end of each bilateral diplomacy period:

| Parameter | What it means |
|---|---|
| **Emissions cut start year** | The year your country begins reducing emissions (2025–2100) |
| **Annual reduction rate (%/yr)** | How fast emissions fall after the start year |
| **Net financial transfer (\$B/yr)** | Positive = you receive; negative = you pay |

Everything else — climate damages, abatement costs, temperature outcome — is calculated by the model from these inputs.

---

# Round Structure

## Opening (before Round 1)

**Coalition caucus (8 min):** Meet with your negotiating group. Develop an opening position. Identify your priority bilateral partners.

**Opening statements (2 min each):** Formal plenary address. State your country's position, priorities, and red lines. This is your public record — what you say here may differ from what you agree to bilaterally.

Presentation order: Climate-Vulnerable States first, then Fossil Fuel Exporters, then Other Developing, then High Emitters, then Emerging Major Emitters.

## Round 1

**Bilateral diplomacy (15 min):** Negotiate freely with any country in any order. Reach bilateral transfer agreements. Discuss start years and reduction rates. No constraints on who talks to whom.

**Proposal submission (last 2 min of bilateral period):** Complete your proposal form. Record your start year, reduction rate, unconditional fallback rate, net transfer, and all bilateral transfer agreements reached.

**Plenary presentations (2 min each):** Present your proposal to the full conference. State any conditions on your commitment clearly and precisely (see Conditionality below).

**Condition audit + model entry:** Secretary General audits conditional pledges. Final proposals entered into the model. Results shown.

## Between Rounds

US shock announced. Review Round 1 model results (Emissions & Temperature and Costs & Damages tabs now visible). 10-minute break.

## Round 2

Same structure as Round 1. Bilateral diplomacy (12 min) → proposal submission → plenary presentations → condition audit → model entry → final results and financial summary revealed.

---

# Conditionality

You may make your commitment conditional on another country's commitment. Conditions are permitted under the following rules:

**Conditions must be specific and verifiable.** You may condition on another country's start year, reduction rate, or transfer amount — parameters that are directly entered into the model. You may not condition on vague political commitments.

> *Permitted:* "India commits to 3%/yr starting 2030, conditional on receiving at least \$12B/yr in net transfers."
>
> *Not permitted:* "India commits to 3%/yr if there is sufficient political will from developed countries."

**One condition per proposal.** You may condition on one other country's parameter only. No chain conditions.

**Submit both rates.** If you make a conditional commitment, your proposal form must include both your conditional rate AND your unconditional fallback rate. The fallback cannot be changed after submission.

**Condition audit before model entry.** After all plenary presentations, the Secretary General audits conditions publicly. If your condition is not met, you choose — out loud, in front of the conference — whether to:

- **Relax and commit:** Accept the deal as it stands and honor your conditional rate anyway.
- **Submit fallback:** Revert to your unconditional fallback rate.

If your fallback triggers another country's unmet condition, that country faces the same choice immediately. Cascades resolve in one pass.

---

# Loss and Damage Moment

Climate-Vulnerable States (Fiji, Marshall Islands) each have one Loss and Damage Moment available for the entire simulation.

**How to invoke:** Announce "Loss and Damage Moment" to the Secretary General at any time during bilateral diplomacy. The Secretary General will call the room to order.

**What it gives you:** 90 seconds of uninterrupted plenary time to state:

1. The temperature outcome you require to sign any agreement
2. The minimum financial transfer you require
3. What your country offers in return

After 90 seconds, bilateral diplomacy resumes. No response from other delegations is required — this is a statement of position, not a negotiation.

**Strategic note:** You have one. Use it when it will have the most impact.

---

# What the Model Shows — and When

| Tab | Visible when |
|---|---|
| Emissions & Temperature | Throughout the simulation |
| Costs & Damages | After Round 1 model reveal |
| Financial Summary (basic) | After Round 1 model reveal |
| Financial Summary (full breakdown) | Debrief only — revealed by instructor |

---

# Proposal Form

*Complete one form per round. Submit to Secretary General before plenary presentations.*

---

**Country:** \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ $\quad$ **Round:** $\square$ 1 $\quad$ $\square$ 2

---

**Emissions Commitment**

| | Start year | Annual reduction rate |
|---|---|---|
| Unconditional fallback | | |
| Conditional (if applicable) | | |

**Condition (if applicable):** \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

---

**Financial Transfer**

Net transfer (+ receive / − pay): \$\_\_\_\_\_\_ B/yr

Bilateral transfer agreements:

| Counterparty | Amount (\$B/yr) | Direction |
|---|---|---|
| | | $\square$ receive $\quad$ $\square$ pay |
| | | $\square$ receive $\quad$ $\square$ pay |
| | | $\square$ receive $\quad$ $\square$ pay |
| | | $\square$ receive $\quad$ $\square$ pay |

---

**Loss and Damage Moment** *(Fiji and Marshall Islands only)*

$\square$ Invoked this round $\quad$ $\square$ Not invoked

---

# Pre-Work Reminder

Before Lecture 13 (April 15):

- Find your country's current NDC on **Climate Action Tracker**
- Translate it into a start year and annual reduction rate — show your math
- The gap between your NDC and the 2°C pathway is your negotiating problem

Before Lecture 14 (April 22):

- Complete your **Negotiation Brief** (due April 19)
- Know your BATNA, your coalition map, and your shock response protocol
